title: Fahrenheit to Celsius
description:
Fahrenheit to Celsius

Given a temperature in Fahrenheit, convert it to Celsius.

Use the formula:

C = (F - 32) × (5 / 9)

Return a floating-point result (Python's normal division is fine).

Examples:

  • Input: fahrenheit = 32
    Output: 0.0
    Explanation: Water freezes at 32°F and 0°C.
  • Input: fahrenheit = 212
    Output: 100.0
  • Input: fahrenheit = -40
    Output: -40.0
    Explanation: -40 is the same on both scales.

Constraints:

  • -459.67 <= fahrenheit <= 1000 (inputs are within ordinary physical / practice ranges)
solution_text:
Fahrenheit to Celsius

Approach:

  1. Subtract 32 from the Fahrenheit value
  2. Multiply by 5 / 9 (use true division so the result is a float)

Time Complexity: O(1)

Space Complexity: O(1)

Key Insights:

  • In Python 3, / always produces a float; // would truncate and break answers
  • Floating-point answers are checked with a small tolerance in the grader
verify:
def verify(actual_output, expected_output):
    if not isinstance(actual_output, (int, float)):
        return [False, str(actual_output)]
    tolerance = 1e-5
    passed = abs(actual_output - expected_output) < tolerance
    output_str = '{:.6f}'.format(actual_output)
    return [passed, output_str]
